subject: Ecology
title: Ocean Zones
description: A fast breakdown of Ocean Zones for builders.

hook.text: The ocean isn't one habitat—it's stacked in layers, each with its own rules of survival.

buildup.text: The sunlit zone reaches 200 meters deep. Almost all photosynthesis happens here.

discovery.text: Below that, the twilight zone gets faint light. The midnight zone gets none at all.

twist.text: The deepest trenches plunge past 10,000 meters—colder, darker, and under crushing pressure.

climax.text: Each zone hosts species found nowhere else, adapted to extreme and specific conditions.

punchline.text: Depth changes everything in the ocean.

quiz.question: Where does most ocean photosynthesis occur?
